purpose: settings.reverse_digraph left at its default False: load_graphml hands the knob to parse_graph (pyreason.py:577) whose reversal branch stays idle (graphml_parser.py:18-19), so the committed fixture's one edge A->B is grounded as-is and 'derived(y) <-1 rel(x,y)' derives derived(B) at t=1 and t=2 with trace-node's grounded-edge column reading [[A,B]] — the twin reverse-digraph-on derives derived(A) over [[B,A]] instead (verified at authoring — the harness judges each case within one knob position); the get_setting readback pins the default False, regrounding keeps the perfect-convergence exit (interpretation.py:674-680) from firing (get_time 3, three frames per filter probe), and edges-rel frames stay empty under default-False save_graph_attributes_to_trace — the default-false-asis and read-at-load-time classes. The pair rides load_graphml deliberately: load_graph never reads the knob (pyreason.py:589-599 carries no reverse_digraph read; grep-verified at the pin), the board's load-path asymmetry.
provenance: spine case against docs/surface.md setting:reverse_digraph default-false-asis + read-at-load-time
